perf.regime: CUTE DSL fp32 RMSNorm on Grace+Blackwell GB10 (sm_121). Memory-bound normalize: one CTA per token row, 128 threads (4 warps), thread-stride over the feature dim (coalesced). Pass 1 = Kahan fp32 sum-of-squares -> block-wide reduce (warp_reduction_sum -> SMEM partials -> thread-0 fold -> math.rsqrt) broadcast via SMEM; Pass 2 = re-walk columns applying x*scale*w. Host upcasts x/w to fp32 (bit-identical to the reference's x.float()) and casts the result back to x.dtype, matching the triton card's fp32-multiply-then-cast order. All reduction primitives confirmed on sm_121 (scripts/ds5_dsl_rowsum_probe.py: warp_reduction_sum + alloc_smem + smem[i] + sync_threads; scripts/ds5_dsl_math_probe2.py: math.rsqrt). fp32-vs-reference agreement is ~1e-6 (Kahan). No matrix engine is involved (this is a reduction, not a GEMM).

measured.source: scripts/ds5_rmsnorm_perf.py (xkernels benchmark/do_bench) on ds5 GB10 sm_121, CTK 13.0, post compile-cache. END-TO-END incl host upcast + 2 single-launch rmsnorm_cute calls (one per latent).

notes:
CUTE DSL (`cutlass.cute`, nvidia-cutlass-dsl 4.5.2) backend for dual_rmsnorm; second native-CUDA card in the library (first REDUCTION-class CUTE card), validated on the ds5 Grace+Blackwell GB10 test bed (docs/ds5-cute-testbed.md). JIT-compiles via nvcc (CUDA_HOME must be set). Registers only if nvidia-cutlass-dsl imports; else degrades to the triton/reference card.

Correctness (2026-06-29): compiled=true; verify 5/5 sweep points PASS on the GPU (correctness.passed=True). max_abs=6.25e-2, max_rel=7.8e-3 — every point's REL error is comfortably inside rtol (fp32 rel ~3e-7 << 1e-5; bf16 rel 7.8e-3 < 0.016); the kernel is correct (fp32-vs-reference agreement ~1e-6 via Kahan). This card FORCED a harness fix: xkernels' verify used the non-standard AND criterion `abs<=atol AND rel<=rtol`, which made atol a magnitude-independent absolute cap and false-failed every point on abs (fp32 atol=1e-6 demands bit-identical fp32; bf16 atol=0.01 < 1 bf16-ULP at |out|>1). Fixed to the standard combined per-element criterion `|a-e| <= atol + rtol*|e|` (numpy/torch/pytest; consistent with the library's own rel-only verify_parity). See verify.py _within_tolerance + docs/ds5-cute-testbed.md. The GEMM card re-verified 5/5 after the fix (no regression — combined is strictly more lenient).

Kernel: each latent (x1/w1/d1, x2/w2/d2) is normalized by an INDEPENDENT launch of the single-row RMSNorm CUTE primitive (rmsnorm_cute). The two norms are numerically independent (the reference dual_rmsnorm_ref does them independently too), so this is bit-faithful. A single fused launch (one CTA covering both latents, as the triton card does) is a perf follow-up, not a correctness concern.

Design (src/xkernels/ops/norm/cute/rmsnorm_kernel.py): pure-fp32 device kernel (host does bf16<->fp32 plumbing, matching the triton card's fp32-multiply-then-cast order); block-per-row; thread-stride coalesced loads; Kahan-compensated sum-of-squares; block-wide reduce via warp_reduction_sum + SMEM partials + sync_threads + math.rsqrt, broadcast via SMEM. Two passes over DRAM (x re-read in pass 2).

Perf (2026-06-29): ms=0.0608 end-to-end at T=64 d1=1536 d2=512 bf16, achieved_bw_pct=4.8 of GB10 ~273 GB/s. The tensor is tiny (0.79MB) + the 2-pass design re-reads x, so this is launch/2-pass-dominated at small T (latency-bound regime), not BW-saturated. Perf follow-ups (not correctness): (a) SMEM x-cache to read x once (halves DRAM traffic); (b) single fused launch for both latents (cuts launch count 2->1); (c) vectorized loads. No matrix engine is involved (reduction, not GEMM).

Compile-cache: per-(T,D) cute.compile handle (tensors-only launch), same pattern as mm_fp8_blockscale.cuda. Device guard raises RuntimeError on CPU tensors so verify_parity (device='cpu') records CUDA as a caught backend error instead of segfaulting.
